description: Account code entered using the Acct soft key during call setup or when connected to an active call.
Average ACOM level, in dB, for the call (ACOM is the combined loss achieved by the echo canceler). 1 indicates that the level cannot be determined or level detection is disabled.
Time at which call is alerting.
(added by the app) This field represents the union of the origAudioCodec and the destAudioCodec
(added by the app) The total kb used for the audio portion of this call leg.
This field provides a description of the Forced Authorization Code.  (part of a rarely used feature to restrict long distance calls to only certain users. )
The raw value of the Forced Authorization Code (FAC) that is associated with the call.  (part of a rarely used feature to restrict long distance calls to only certain users. )
The level of the Forced Authorization Code (FAC) applicable to the call.  (part of a rarely used feature to restrict long distance calls to only certain users. )
Sent in stop accounting messages for call legs 1 and 4. Also included in interim-update packets.

(added by the app) This field has values of either 'business_hours' or 'off_hours'. By default it considers business hours to be 8am to 5pm in the timezone of the splunk server. This can be edited by going to 'Settings > Fields > Calculated Fields'  and finding the 'bh_type' field in that list, but you can also contact Sideview support for help.
Total number of bytes received.
Total number of transmitted bytes.
(added by the app) (DEPRECATED) This was a field used in older versions of the app, however it proved to be more misleading than it was useful.
(added by the app) (DEPRECATED) This was a field used in older versions of the app, however it proved to be more misleading than it was useful.

(added by the app) (DEPRECATED) This was a field used in older versions of the app, however it proved to be more misleading than it was useful.
(added by the app) (DEPRECATED) This was a field used in older versions of the app, however it proved to be more misleading than it was useful.
(added by the app) (DEPRECATED) This was a field used in older versions of the app, however it proved to be more misleading than it was useful.

(added by the app) This field will have a value of 0 for any call that connected without error but also for any call that didnt connect for certain relatively normal reasons like unassigned-number. Then for any call that failed for any bad reason, like network error or capacity reasons or unexpected reasons, it will have a value of 0. Note that since this field is on the call-legs, multileg calls may have BOTH 0 and 1 as values, so splitting by or grouping by this field may well count such calls on both sides.

(added by the app) In different data types this will take different values. in CUCM CDR this field is the concatenated values of the 3 fields globalCallID_callId, globalCallID_callManagerId and globalCallId_ClusterID. In CUBE data this is just the value of the Call-ID header.
(added by the app) A field that is extracted from part of the ProtocolCallRef fields, and used to correlate UCM CDR with CUBE CDR data.
(added by the app) This is an alias that attempts to hold the best name of the (final) called party.

(added by the app) This is a general field holding the value or values of the called party's extension or DN.  EG: for CUCM CDR this is the finalCalledPartyNumber and for CUBE CDR this is the dnis field.
This is an integer code indicating the pattern of the called party. Added by cisco for hunt list support.

(added by the app) This represents the short description of the particular calledPartyPatternUsage integer code. Default value specifies 5 (PATTERN_ROUTE). If the huntPilotDN field is populated, the huntPilotDN field value is the hunt pilot. If the huntPilotDN is not available, and the pattern usage is 7 (PATTERN_HUNT_PILOT), then the this call is a hunt list call and the finalCalledPartyNumber represents the huntPilotDN.
(added by the app) This represents the all-caps 'name' of the particular calledPartyPatternUsage integer code. Useful for hunt list analysis.
(added by the app) this is deprecated in favor of the call_id field.
For calls with more than one call leg (thus more than one CDR), this identifies the call leg for the given record

Best-fit calling party category value extracted from the Generic Transparency Descriptor (GTD). Sent in start and stop accounting messages for call legs 1 and 4. Optionally, this field also contains a 3-character country code representing the country variant extracted from the GTD Protocol Name (PRN) country field or the national value extracted from the GTD Field Compatibility Information (FDC) data field.
(added by the app)  This gives the area code of the callingParty in inbound calls.
(added by the app)  This gives the city of the callingParty in inbound calls.
(added by the app)  This identifies the CLLI (Common Language Location Identifier) of the callingParty in inbound calls from US numbers.
(added by the app)  This identifies the company name that the US area code and exchange is associated with, if any.
(added by the app)  This identifies the company type of the callingParty in inbound calls as one of CAP, CLEC, GENERAL, ICO, L RESELLER, PCS, RBOC, ULEC, UNKNOWN, W RESELLER, WIRELESS.
(added by the app)  This gives the country name of the callingParty in inbound calls. This is determined solely by the value extracted from the callingPartyNumber for callingPartyCountryCode.
(added by the app)  This gives the countryCode of the callingParty in inbound calls.
(added by the app)  This gives the exchange of the callingParty in inbound calls within or from the US.

(added by the app) This will contain the department or group in which the callingPartyNumber is listed.  This field is one of those created by the groups lookup, which runs automatically once created.  See 'App Settings > Groups and extensions' to learn more.

(added by the app) This gives the latitude of the calling party.  Depending on whether this is an internal or external party, it might be pulled from the Sites lookup via cidr-matching the IP Address, or it might be pulled from the country as recognized by the parsed callingPartyCountryCode.
(added by the app) This gives the LATA of the callingParty in inbound calls.

(added by the app) This gives the longitude of the calling party.  Depending on whether this is an internal or external party, it might be pulled from the Sites lookup via cidr-matching the IP Address, or it might be pulled from the country as recognized by the parsed callingPartyCountryCode.

(added by the app) This is an alias that attempts to hold the best name of the calling party. In CUCM, if the deviceName is in the devices lookup this will be the device's associated username. Failing that this will be the name associated with the extension in the groups lookup. In other data types this alias may also be present and pulled from different fields.

Number or extension of the party originating the call. For calls that originate at a Cisco Unified IP Phone, this field shows the extension number of the line that is used. For incoming H.323 calls, this field specifies the value that is received in the Calling Party Number field in the Setup message. This field reflects any translations that are applied to the Calling Party Number before it arrives at the Cisco Unified Communications Manager (such as translations at the gateway).For server calls, where Cisco Unified Communications Manager originates a half call without a calling party, this field may remain empty.

Identifies the partition name that is associated with the CallingPartyNumber field because Cisco Unified Communications Manager supports multiple Cisco Unified IP Phones with the same extension number in different partitions. For calls that ingress through an H.323 gateway, this field remains blank.

(created by the app) If it can be determined by the libphonenumbers library, this will identify whether the callingPartyNumber is a fixed line, toll free, mobile number, shared cost, etc..  Contact support@sideviewapps.com if you would like more details
(added by the app)  This identifies the OCN (Operating Company Number) of the callingParty in inbound calls.
(added by the app)  This gives the state of the callingParty in inbound calls from US parties, if it can be determined from area code and exchange.
(added by the app)  This gives the two letter abbreviation of the US state of the callingParty in inbound calls from US parties, if it can be determined from area code and exchange.

(created by the app) If the groups lookup has been configured to). See the documentation under Setup>Groups. If you set up the groups lookup to have this field in addition to callingPartyGroup, then this field will exist

The calling party login user ID. If the user ID does not exist, this field stays empty. NOTE: YOUR DATA MAY NOT HAVE ANY VALUES FOR THIS FIELD. IF THAT IS THE CASE THESE EXAMPLE REPORTS WILL NOT WORK.
(added by the app) This gives the zipcode of the callingParty in inbound calls from within the US.
(added by the app) This represents the union of both the origCallingSearchSpace and destCallingSearchSpace fields.
Indicates whether the given call leg is from an audio or video call.

The highest security status that is reached during a call. For example, if the call is originally unsecured, then later the call changes to secured, the CDR contains 1 for 'Secured' even though different portions of the call have different status values. (0 - Non-secured, 1 - Authenticated (not encrypted), 2 - Secured (encrypted)
ISUP carrier ID.

(added by the app) This field will be set to the value of destCause_value or origCause_value, whichever is nonzero.  This is part of how the app determines the overall call release cause code for the call leg - see cause_description.
(added by the app) This field is the english description of the overall termination cause code. Specifically, it represents the description of whichever of the two integer cause codes is nonzero
Cumulative Conceal Ratio -- the cumulative ratio of concealment time over speech time that is observed after starting a call.
Determines CDR template used:0=None, 1=Call history detail, 2=Custom template.
Charge number used for call.
Number of charged units for this connection. For incoming calls or if charging information is not supplied by the switch, the value is zero.

(added by the app) Created from the app's sites lookup (aka cidr.csv), this field will hold the union of origCidr and destCidr, which are the subnets that were used to match the origSite and destSite fields respectively.
(added by the app) this is a guid that can be used in some advanced cases to follow links to/from downstream and upstream voice systems.
Calling number.
Before the system extends a call, the user enters a client matter code that can be used for assigning account or billing codes to calls. This field displays the client matter code.
(added by the app) Created by the app's cluster lookup, this field can be used to specify a more recognizable name than the value found in globalCallId_ClusterID.

(added by the app) This can be set per cluster in the clusters lookup (see App Settings > Clusters and locales).  It is  exposed as a field in the UI mostly for troubleshooting although it might be of some use in advanced reporting on very large UCM deployments.
(added by the app) Created by the app's cluster lookup, this field can be used to specify a more recognizable name than the value found in globalCallId_ClusterID.

(modified by the app) This reflects the duration field as reported by the CMR (Call Management Record), as opposed to the much more familiar duration as reported by the CDR (call Detail Record).  We are not quite sure what, if anything,  this can reliably represent.  For calls placed on hold, there does seem to be an additional CMR created where this value seems to closely match the hold duration but it's possible Cisco uses this field for other corner cases as well. Note that in the raw CDR files the field is called simply duration. In this app we rename this to cmr_duration so as to avoid conflicts and conflations with the actual call duration field.
Payload size of the voice packet.
Negotiated coder rate. Transmit rate of voice/fax compression to its associated call leg for the call.

This field allows features to add text to the CDRs. This text can describe details about the call. For example, the following field flags malicious calls: Tag-CallFlag Value-MALICIOUS. The features in question may also have written semi-colon separated lists of additional fields here.

(added by the app) This field is calculated on the fly by the app and represents the number of concurrent calls at the given instant. However this field has some unusual behavior worth noting.  First it is not any kind of global concurrency but rather the concurrency the context of your current search results.  When you have this field active in Investigate Calls it represents the number of other concurrent calls that were connected at the moment that the given call started.  In the Chart page you can create reports of max concurrency over time where it presents a complete picture of the concurrency over the life of the calls however note that in the Chart page it can only be used with the 'max' statistic, so you must first set the pulldown to 'max' to use it at all.
(added by the app) In some conference calls, this field represents the deviceName of the party that is controlling the overall call
(added by the app) In some conference calls, this field represents the DN of the party that is controlling the overall call
(added by the app) In some conference calls, this field represents the deviceName of the party that added a new participant
(added by the app) In some conference calls, this field represents the DN of the party that added a new participant
(added by the app) This field is the union of origConversationId and destConversation. UCM uses these to denote chained conference bridge calls.
Concealed Secs represents the time during which some concealment is observed during a call.
(added by the app) This is calculated from the CS values and is just the total CS across all of the given call's CMR data. When the call has only one CMR then CS_total and CS are the same.

(added by the app) this is the CS Ratio, in other words the seconds of concealed speech divided by the total number of seconds. Note that this is calculated by the app and not on the individual CMR records but across all CMR's for the given call, as a single statistic on the call. It is calculated by taking CS_total and dividing by duration_total.
It is not actually clear to us what this field is, nor can we find Cisco docs about it.
This is the duration of a cube leg
It is not actually clear to us what this field is, nor can we find Cisco docs about it.
This field is used with CUCM's external call control feature and represents the reason why the call was intercepted for the current call.
(added by the app) This is the name corresponding to the currentRoutingReason code.  ie currentRoutingReason 5 corresponds to PDPDecision_Forward_Fulfilled.
SIP business group ID.
(added by the app) This indicates whether the given record is a CUCM record (CDR or CMR) or whether it is a CUBE record.  Note in future releases other values will become possible.
The date and time that the call connects. The time gets stored as an epochtime integer. If the call is never answered, this value shows zero.
The date and time when the call is cleared. This field gets set even if the call never connects. The time gets stored as an epochtime integer

Only in CDR rows - the date and time when the user goes off hook or the date and time that the H.323 Setup message is received for an incoming call. epochtime-valued, (integer number of seconds since 1/1/1970 as measured in GMT).
Only in CMR rows - the approximate time that the device goes on hook. Cisco Unified Communications Manager records the time when the phone responds to a request for diagnostic information.
(added by the app) This field represents the day of the month as calculated in the timezone of the Splunk server. Values are zeropadded
(added by the app) This field represents the day of the week as calculated in the timezone of the Splunk server. Values are Sun,Mon,Tue,Wed,Thu,Fri,Sat
(added by the app) This field represents the day of the year as calculated in the timezone of the Splunk server. Values are integers from 1 to 366
(added by the app) A flag that may or may not be present on an MGCP gateway value, if present indicating that the type of endpoint is analog
(added by the app) When destination devices involve UCCX this will contain the destination route point.

(added by the app) This gives you the type of device receiving the call. Example values are hardphone, jabberphone, softphone, uccx. If the device does not match one of the extractions in transforms.conf then the field will be undefined.
(added by the app) This is the name of the gateway used in the outbound call
(added by the app) One of the components of how MGCP gateways are listed - taken together port slot and sub_unit define an individual interface on the given MGCP gateway.
(added by the app) One of the components of how MGCP gateways are listed - taken together port slot and sub_unit define an individual interface on the given MGCP gateway.
(added by the app) One of the components of how MGCP gateways are listed - taken together port slot and sub_unit define an individual interface on the given MGCP gateway.
(added by the app) this is the CIM-compliant version of the destIPAddr field.
(added by the app) The MGCP gateway name itself, stripped of the AALN, port slot and sub_unit components.
(added by the app) this is the mac address extracted from destination hardphone devices.
Specifies the bandwidth in kbps of the destination device's codec, if known. (to check manually, look at the calls destAudioCodec and then look that row up in codec_types.csv within the app)
(added by the app) Represents the audio codec used by the destination device.
(added by the app) This field is extracted by default from devices whose names begin with CCG.
(added by the app) If the destDeviceName value is present in the devices lookup, this field will be the callingSearchSpace listed for that device.

Call release cause code specifying why the destination was terminated. For example, if the originator of the call hangs up the phone, the OnBehalfOf code shows '12' for Device. If the call terminates because of a transfer, the OnBehalfOf code shows '10' for Transfer.
(added by the app) The readable name of the reason why the destination was terminated. See the field 'destCallTerminationOnBehalfOf'.
(added by the app) This field is the english description of the appropriate call release cause code for calls cleared by the destination party
For clearing causes that are received over ISDN signaling links, the ISDN release message indicates this location field.
For calls that are cleared by the destination party, this integer reflects the reason for clearance. For calls that are cleared by the originating party, this value will be zero.

(added by the app) For calls whose destDeviceName matches one of the subnets in the Sites lookup,   this field holds the value of the matching subnet.  This can be useful when you have multiple subnets mapped to a single sites,   but where the individual subnets themselves remain occasionally interesting. You can add and edit subnets in the Sites lookup under 'App Settings >  Sites'

A unique identifier that is used to identify the parties of a conference call. For conference chaining scenarios, the origConversationId and destConversationId fields identify which conferences are chained together.
(added by the app) If the destDeviceName value is present in the devices lookup, this field will be the description listed for that device.
(added by the app) If the destDeviceName value is present in the devices lookup, this field will be the primary (or failing that secondary) DN or DiD number associated with the destination device.
The name of the destination device, whether it's a soft phone, physical phone or gateway device.
(added by the app) If the destDeviceName value is present in the devices lookup, this field will be the devicePool listed for that device.
(added by the app) If the destDeviceName value is present in the devices lookup, this field will be the protocol listed for that device.

The DTMF method that the destination uses. (0 - No DTMF - Use ANY matched DTMF. 1 - OOB - Use OOB if endpoints behind SIPTrunk support it. 2 - 2833 - Use RFC2833 if endpoints behind SIPTrunk support it. 3 - OOB and 2833 - Use both KPML and RFC2833 if endpoints behind SIPTrunk can support both. 4 - Unknown.  See field destDTMFMethod_description  also.
(added by the app) The human readable version of the field destDTMFMethod.
(added by the app) Extracted from the destination device names that match the 'cisco-cdr-desthardphone' regex in transforms.conf, this represents the username of the destination hardphone.

(modified by the app) This field identifies the IP address of the device that terminates the call signaling. For Cisco Unified IP Phones, this field specifies the address of the phone. For PSTN calls, this field specifies the address of the H.323 gateway. For intercluster calls, this field specifies the address of the remote Cisco Unified Communications Manager. Although present in the CDR as an integer in most callmanager versions, the Sideview app automatically converts these values to real IP addresses.
(added by the app) Extracted from the destination device names that match the 'cisco-cdr-destipcom' regex in transforms.conf, this represents the username of the destination IP Communicator device.
This field is sometimes present and when UCCE or UCCX is one of the parties on the call it will be populated with an IP Address from UCCX/UCCE.
(added by the app) Extracted from the destination device names that match the 'cisco-cdr-destjabberphone' regex in transforms.conf, this represents the username of the destination Jabber device.

This field identifies the terminating leg of a call. This value remains unique within a cluster. If the leg of a call persists across several sub-calls and, consequently, several CDRs (as during a call transfer), this value remains constant.
The media bandwidth that is used at the destination of the call.
This field is not reported by CUCM
The number of milliseconds of data per packet that the terminating party of the call sends. This field normally gets set to 10, 20, or 30 for G.729 or G.711 codecs but can store any nonzero value.
The codec type that the terminating party uses to transmit media. Cisco Unified Communications Manager currently uses the following payload capability values: 0, 1-16, 18-20, 25, 32, 33, 81-86.

The IP address of the device that terminates the media for the call. For Cisco Unified IP Phones, this field designates the address of the phone. For PSTN calls, this field designates the address of the H.323 gateway. For intercluster calls, this field shows the address of the remote phone.
IP port number that is associated with the DestMediaTransportAddress_IP field.
For mobility calls, specifies the destination mobile device's call duration.
For mobility calls, specifies the destination mobile device name.
The location, or node within a cluster, to which the terminating party of the call is registered at the time that the call is made.

For MLPP, each call leg has a precedence level. This field represents the destination legs precedence level.(0 = FLASH OVERRIDE, 1 = FLASH, 2 = IMMEDIATE, 3 = PRIORITY,4 = ROUTINE).  See field destPrecedenceLevel_description also.
(added by the app) The human readable version of the destPrecendenceLevel.  FLASH OVERRIDE, EXECUTIVE OVERRIDE, FLASH, IMMEDIATE, PRIORITY, ROUTINE
(added by the app) If the destDeviceName value is present in the devices lookup, this field will be the product name listed for that device.
The status of the RSVP audio reservation from terminator to originator.  See field origRSVPAudioStat_description for more information and a human readable version of these values.

(added by the app) The human readable version of the Status of the RSVP audio reservation from terminator to originator.  0 - No reservation,1 - RSVP Reservation Failure condition at call setup or feature invocation, 2  - RSVP Reservation Success condition at call setup or feature invocation,3 - RSVP Reservation No Response (RSVP Agent) condition at call setup or feature invocation,4 - RSVP Mid Call Failure Preempted condition (preempted after call setup),5 - RSVP Mid Call Failure Lost Bandwidth condition (includes all mid-call failures except MLPP preemption).
The status of the RSVP video reservation from terminator to originator.  See field origRSVPVideoStat_description for more information and a human readable version of these values.

(added by the app) The human readable version of the Status of the RSVP video reservation from terminator to originator.  0 - No reservation,1 - RSVP Reservation Failure condition at call setup or feature invocation, 2  - RSVP Reservation Success condition at call setup or feature invocation,3 - RSVP Reservation No Response (RSVP Agent) condition at call setup or feature invocation,4 - RSVP Mid Call Failure Preempted condition (preempted after call setup),5 - RSVP Mid Call Failure Lost Bandwidth condition (includes all mid-call failures except MLPP preemption).
(added by the app) If the destDeviceName value is present in the devices lookup, this field will be the security profile listed for that device.

(added by the app) This field is derived from matching the destIpAddr against the subnets specified in the app's Sites lookup.  You can add and edit subnets in the Sites lookup under 'App Settings > Sites'

For calls that are received at a gateway, this field indicates the B channel number of the T1, PRI, or BRI trunk where the call is received, or a zero value for FXS or FXO trunks. For H.323 gateways, the span number remains unknown, and this field contains the call leg ID of the destination. For calls not terminating at a gateway, the value specifies zero. If the destination cannot be reached, this field stays 0.
(added by the app) Extracted from the destination device names that match the 'cisco-cdr-destuccx' regex in transforms.conf, this represents the username of the destination UCCX device.
(added by the app) Extracted from the destination device names that match the 'cisco-cdr-destunityvm' regex in transforms.conf, this represents the username of the destination Unity Voicemail device.
(added by the app) If the devices lookup has been set up (Generally with Sideview's Supporting Add On for Cisco AXL), this will populate with the department of the given party
(added by the app) If the devices lookup has been set up (Generally with Sideview's Supporting Add On for Cisco AXL), this will populate with the email address of the given party
(added by the app) If the devices lookup has been set up (Generally with Sideview's Supporting Add On for Cisco AXL), this will populate with the userId  of the given party
(added by the app) If the devices lookup has been set up (Generally with Sideview's Supporting Add On for Cisco AXL), this will populate with the userName of the given party
Specifies the bandwidth in kbps of the destination device's codec, if known. (to check manually, look at the call's destVideoCodec and then look that row up in codec_types.csv within the app)
Bandwidth measured in kbps.

This field identifies the bandwidth measured in units of kbps for the second video channel. Default - 0. If media does not get established, this field displays 0. Also, if H.239 is not supported, this field displays 0.
The codec type that the terminating party uses to transmit video (100 = H.261, 101 = H.263,102 = Video).

This field identifies the codec type that the terminating party uses to transmit video for the second video channel (H.261, H.263, Vieo, H.264). Default - 0. If media does not get established, this field displays 0. Also, if H.239 is not supported, this field displays 0.

Indicates the transmitting resolution. In the case of H.264 codec or SIP device, this field refers to the max transmitting resolution the device can transmit for this call. See field destVideoCap_Resolution_description for more information and human-readable values.

Indicates the transmitting resolution for the second video channel. In the case of H.264 codec or SIP device, this field refers to the maximum transmitting resolution the device can transmit for this call.  \Default - 0. If media is not established, this field stays 0. Also, if H.239 and BFCP are not supported for this call, this field displays 0.   For more information and human-readable values, see field destVideoCap_Resolution_Channel2_description

(added by the app) This field identifies the video resolution for the second video channel. (0 = Nothing/default, 1 = SQCIF, 2 = QCIF, 3 = CIF, 4 = CIF4, 5 = CIF16, 6 = H263 custom resolution, 7 = W360P, 8 = VGA, 9 = W448P, 10 = HD720P, 11 = HD1080P, 12 = CIF2 )

(added by the app) The human-readable version of the video resolution. (0 = Nothing/default, 1 = SQCIF, 2 = QCIF, 3 = CIF, 4 = CIF4, 5 = CIF16, 6 = H263 custom resolution, 7 = W360P, 8 = VGA, 9 = W448P, 10 = HD720P, 11 = HD1080P, 12 = CIF2 )

This field identifies the H.239 video channel role of the device that receives the call. Default - 0. If media does not get established, this field displays 0. Also, if H.239 is not supported, this field displays 0.
For video calls, represents the codec used by the destination device. For audio calls this field will be empty.
The IP address of the device that receives the call.

This field identifies the v4 IP address of the device that receives the call. Default - 0. If media does not get established, this field displays 0. Also, if H.239 is not supported, this field displays 0
The video RTP port that is associated with the destVideoTransportAddress_IP field.

This field identifies the video RTP port associated with the destH239VideoTransportAddress_IP field. Default - 0. If media does not get established, this field displays 0. Also, if H.239 is not supported, this field displays 0.
(added by the app) A useful field for broad filtering and reporting by device_name, this field is generally multivalue and contains both the origDeviceName value and the destDeviceName value.
(added by the app) This field will combine the values of orig_device_type and dest_device_type, and if both are defined, it will have both values (ie it will be a multivalue field).
(added by the app) This represents the union of both the origDeviceClassName and destDeviceClassName fields.
(added by the app) This represents the union of both the origDeviceDescription and destDeviceDescription fields.
(added by the app) This represents the union of both the origDeviceDirectoryNumber and destDeviceDirectoryNumber fields.
The deviceName reported in the CMR.  This may be useful in some corner cases, but you should probably look at origDeviceName, destDeviceName and device_name, which are the fields from the CDR.
(added by the app) This represents the union of both the origDevicePool and destDevicePool fields.
(added by the app) This represents the union of both the origDeviceProtocol and destDeviceProtocol fields.
(added by the app) This represents the union of both the origDeviceSubclassName and destDeviceSubclassName fields.

Present only in the CMR, this field specifies the directory number of the device from which the given call diagnostics are collected. Do not use as a substitute for callingPartyNumber, finalCalledPartyNumber etc..

Present only in the CMR, this identifies the partition of the directory number from which the given call diagnostics are collected. May be empty if no partition exists.  Do not use as a substitute for callingPartyNumberPartition,  finalCalledPartyNumberPartition, etc.
ASCII text describing the reason for call termination.
Called number.
(added by the app) In some conference calls, this field represents the DN of the party that removed/dropped a given participant
(added by the app) In some conference calls, this field represents the deviceName of the party that removed/dropped a given participant
Useful in verifying the DSCP value of the received video stream marked by endpoint.
Useful in verifying the DSCP value of the received video stream marked by endpoint.
DSP ID used for the current call.

The difference between the Connect Time and Disconnect Time. This field is defined on each call leg, and specifies the time that the call leg remained connected, in seconds. This field remains zero if the call never connects or if it connects for less than 1 second.  NOTE: For calls with multiple legs, statistics like average duration will treat each call leg of each call separately and as such may give unexpected results. Always consider whether you need the duration, duration_total or duration_elapsed field for your report
This field is specified for each call, and it specifies the number of seconds from the connect_time of the earliest call leg to connect,  to the disconnect time of the last call leg to disconnect.
(added by the app) This is just the duration in seconds, divided by 60 and rounded to two decimal places. See duration for more details.

This field is specified for each call and specifies the sum of the duration of the individual call legs comprising that call, in seconds.   If the call legs are all exactly in sequence, it will be a very strong intuitive 'length of the call' measure. For conference calls however, note that this field value will in general be much longer than the 'duration_elapsed' field value.
Number of received voice packets that arrived too early to store in the jitter buffer during the call.
(added by the app) This represents the party numbers that are actually external DN's,  ie on the external side of the call.
Whether a fax was originated (transmit) or terminated (receive) by this gateway.
Whether error correction mode is enabled.
Encapsulation protocol used for fax transfer.
Whether fax transfer was successful, the transfer failed, or indeterminate.
Initial high-speed modulation and baud rate negotiated at the time the call is connected.
Number of jitter buffer overflow events during the call.
Depth of the jitter buffer, in ms.
Most recent high-speed modulation and baud rate.
NSF country code of local fax device; country name per T.35, Annex A.
NSF manufacturer code of local fax device.
Sum of the number of transmitted and received fax pages.
Packet loss concealment; number of white scan lines inserted (nonzero for outbound gateway only).
Number of packets received.
Fax start time in a call. There can be multiple fax start/stop time stamps in one call. Recorded for both VoIP and telephony call legs.
Fax stop time in a call. There can be multiple fax start/stop time stamps in one call. Recorded for both VoIP and telephony call legs.
Number of packets transmitted.
Feature ID of the invocation. Identifies a unique instance of a feature attribute within a gateway. This number is incremented for each added feature attribute.
Feature name. Two-Way Call (TWC), Call Forward All (CFA), Call Forward Busy (CFBY), Call Forward No Answer (CFNA), Blind Transfer (BXFER), Consultive Transfer (CXFER), Hold (HOLD), Resume (RESUME).

Used in conjunction with feature_id_field1.  When feature_id_field1=TWC, this is not used.  When CFA, CFNA or CFBY it is the forwarded number (fwdee).  When BXFER or CXFER, it is the transferred from party (Xor or transferor).  When HOLD or RESUME it is whether the line is shared or not shared (sl).

Used in conjunction with feature_id_field1.  When feature_id_field1=TWC, this is not used.  When CFA, CFNA or CFBY it is the forwarded to number (fwdto).  When BXFER or CXFER, it is the transferred number (Xee).  When HOLD or RESUME it is the username associated with the phone that initiated the hold or resume (usr).

Used in conjunction with feature_id_field1.  When feature_id_field1=TWC, this is not used.  When CFA, CFNA or CFBY it is the forwarding from number (frm).  When BXFER or CXFER, it is the transferred to party (Xto). When HOLD or RESUME it is the ephone tag of the SCCP phone if the usr field is blank (tag)
Feature invocation time.
Used in conjunction with feature_id_field1.  When feature_id_field1=TWC, this is the calling number.  For all other values of feature_id_field1, this is the feature status (frs).
Used in conjunction with feature_id_field1.  When feature_id_field1=TWC, this is the called number.  For all other values of feature_id_field1, this is the feature ID (fid).
Used in conjunction with feature_id_field1.  When feature_id_field1=TWC, this is the feature status (frs).  For all other values of feature_id_field1, this is the feature correlation id (fcid).
Used in conjunction with feature_id_field1.  When feature_id_field1=TWC, this is the feature ID (fid).  When CFA, CFRNA, CFBY, HOLD, or RESUME it is the legID.  When BXFER or CXFER, it's the XconsID.

Used in conjunction with feature_id_field1.  When feature_id_field1=TWC, this is the feature correlation id (fcid).  When CFA, CFNA or CFBY it is the forwarding reason (frson).  When BXFER or CXFER, it is the legID. When HOLD or RESUME is it the hold reason (hrson).

Used in conjunction with feature_id_field1.  When feature_id_field1=TWC, this is the legID.  When CFA, CFNA or CFBY it is the forwarding count (fdcnt).  When BXFER or CXFER, it is the forwarding reason (frson). When HOLD or RESUME is it the Directory number of the user who placed the call on hold (holding).

Used in conjunction with feature_id_field1.  When feature_id_field1=TWC, this is not used.  When CFA, CFNA or CFBY it is the forwarded from number (fwder). When BXFER or CXFER, it is the forwarding reason (frson).  When HOLD or RESUME it is the directory number of the caller on hold (held).
Success (0) or failure (1).
Feature operation time. Time stamp of the operation start and stop time, if applicable for a given feature.
Feature operation.
(added by the app)  This gives the area code of the calledParty in outbound calls.
(added by the app)  This gives the city of the finalCalledParty in outbound calls.
(added by the app)  This identifies the CLLI (Common Language Location Identifier) of the finalCalledParty in outbound calls to US numbers.
(added by the app)  This identifies the company name that the US area code and exchange is associated with, if any.
(added by the app)  This identifies the company type of the finalCalledParty in outbound calls as one of CAP, CLEC, GENERAL, ICO, L RESELLER, PCS, RBOC, ULEC, UNKNOWN, W RESELLER, WIRELESS.

(added by the app)  This gives the country code of the calledParty in outbound calls. This is determined solely by the value that was extracted from finalCalledPartyNumber for finalCalledPartyCountryCode.
(added by the app)  This gives the country code of the calledParty in outbound calls.
(added by the app)  This gives the exchange of the calledParty in outbound calls.

(added by the app) This will contain the department or group in which the finalCalledPartyNumber is listed.  This field is one of those created by the groups lookup, which runs automatically once created.  See 'App Settings > Groups and extensions' to learn more.

(added by the app) This gives the latitude of the called party.  Depending on whether this is an internal or external party, it might be pulled from the Sites lookup via cidr-matching the IP Address, or it might be pulled from the country as recognized by the parsed finalCalledPartyCountryCode.
(added by the app) This gives the LATA of the finalCalledParty in outbound calls.

(added by the app) This gives the longitude of the called party.  Depending on whether this is an internal or external party, it might be pulled from the Sites lookup via cidr-matching the IP Address, or it might be pulled from the country as recognized by the parsed finalCalledPartyCountryCode.

(added by the app) This will contain the name of the person with the given finalCalledPartyNumber  This field is one of those created by the groups lookup, which runs automatically once created.  See 'App Settings > Groups and extensions'  to learn more.

Number or extension to which the call finally gets presented, until it is answered or rings out. If no forwarding occurs, this number shows the same number as the originalCalledPartyNumber. For calls to a conference bridge, this field contains the actual identifier of the conference bridge, which is an alphanumeric string (for example, b0019901001).

Identifies the partition name that is associated with the FinalCalledPartyNumber field because Cisco Unified Communications Manager supports multiple Cisco Unified IP Phones with the same extension number in different partitions. For calls that egress through an H.323 gateway, this field uniquely specifies the partition name that is associated with the route pattern that points to the gateway.

(created by the app) If it can be determined by the libphonenumbers library, this will identify whether the finalCalledPartyNumber is a fixed line, toll free, mobile number, shared cost, etc..  Contact support@sideviewapps.com if you would like more details
(added by the app)  This identifies the OCN (Operating Company Number) of the finalCalledParty in outbound calls.
This is the pattern matched by the finalCalledPartyNumber.
(added by the app)  This gives the US state of the finalCalledParty in outbound calls to US numbers, if it can be determined from area code and exchange.
(added by the app)  This gives the two letter abbreviation of the US state of the finalCalledParty in outbound calls to US numbers, if it can be determined from area code and exchange.

(created by the app, If the groups lookup has been configured to). See the documentation under Setup>Groups. If you set up the groups lookup to have this field in addition to finalCalledPartyGroup, then this field will exist

The final called party field specifies the login user ID. If the user ID does not exist, this field stays empty. NOTE: YOUR DATA MAY NOT HAVE ANY VALUES FOR THIS FIELD. IF THAT IS THE CASE THESE EXAMPLE REPORTS WILL NOT WORK.
(added by the app)  This gives the zipcode of the finalCalledParty in outbound calls to numbers within the US.
Critical to SNR (Single Number Reach), this field holds the mobile number to which the call ultimately goes to.

(added by the app) Only present in particular views). This field is created by the app in certain views and represents a fairly intelligent merging of location information deduced from the country code,  area code and exchange,  with other information deduced from the IP addresses and relevant subnet from the sites lookup. Users wishing to use this field in their own searches and reports can use this combination of macros to construct the field manually in their data - `get_sites` | `get_locations` | `get_to_and_from`

Duration, in ms, of the voice signal played out with the signal synthesized from parameters or samples of data preceding and following in time because of voice data not received on time (or lost) from the voice gateway for this call.

Duration, in ms, of voice signal played out with signal synthesized from parameters or samples of data preceding in time because of voice data not received on time (or lost) from voice gateway for this call.
Duration, in ms, of the voice signal played out with signal synthesized from redundancy parameters available because of voice data not received on time (or lost) from the voice gateway for this call.
Duration, in ms, of the voice signal replaced with the signal played out during silence because of voice data not received on time (or lost) from the voice gateway for this call
(added by the app) This field holds the names of the gateways used in the call, and is essentially the union of the orig_gateway and dest_gateway fields.
Called number presented by the gatekeeper in the ACF RAS message. GK/GKTMP could modify the called number by appending a prefix or leave it unchanged.
Calling number presented by the gatekeeper in the ACF RAS message. The GK/GKTMP could modify the calling number which is carried in the ACF nonstandard parameter.

Although you can think of this as a unique ID for calls, the 'global' is a misnomer in that this field is technically only unique within a given globalCallId_ClusterID and within a given globalCallID_callManagerID
Represents the CM server/node associated with the call.
A unique ID that identifies a cluster of Cisco Unified Communications Managers. The field is generated at installation and is not used by Cisco Unified Communications Manager.

(added by the app) This field represents the union of the callingPartyGroup, originalCalledPartyGroup and finalCalledPartyGroup, as looked up in the groups lookup, for the given call leg or for all call legs in the given call.
GTD connected number.
Original calling number received by the gateway.
Originating carrier identification code, used in routing to identify the network.
Terminating carrier identification code.
Destination number collected by the gateway (application) that is used to route the call. Only applicable for 2-stage calls.
Called number to be sent out of the gateway.
Calling number to be sent out of the gateway.
Final translated received number.
Called number received in the incoming signaling message before any translation rules are applied.
Calling number received in the incoming signaling message before any translation rules are applied.
Redirection number received by the gateway.
Gateway's behavior in relation to the connection that is active for this leg.  Answer= Legs 1 and 3, originate= Legs 2 and 4, callback = Legs 1 and 3.
Unique call identifier generated by the gateway. Used to identify the separate billable events (calls) within a single calling session.
Connect time in NTP format: hour, minutes, seconds, microseconds, time_zone, day, month, day_of_month, year.
Q.931 disconnect cause code retrieved from Cisco IOS call-control application programming interface (Cisco IOS CCAPI).
Disconnect time in NTP format: hour, minutes, seconds, microseconds, time_zone, day, month, day_of_month, year.
AV pairs sent from the voice gateway to the RADIUS server that you can define. You can set (write) the value with a customized Tcl IVR script.
Setup time in Network Time Protocol (NTP) format: hour, minutes, seconds, microseconds, time_zone, day, month, day_of_month, year.

Value representing impairment/calculated planning impairment factor (ICPIF) of the voice quality on the connection provided by lower-layer drivers (such as the digital-signal-processor). Low numbers represent better quality.
High-water mark voice playout FIFO delay during the voice call.
(added by the app) This field represents the hour of the day as calculated in the timezone of the Splunk server. Values are two digit numbers eg:  00,01,.... 22,23

(modified by the app) This may be reliably present in the CDR directly.  However if the huntPilotDN field is not populated, and the calledPartyPatternUsage is 7, the app will set this field to the value of the finalCalledPartyNumber, as per Cisco's docs on the matter.

(added by the app) This will contain the department or group that is listed for the huntPilotDN, if any.  This field is one of those created by the groups lookup, which runs automatically once created.  See 'App Settings > Groups and extensions' to learn more.

(added by the app) This will contain the name that is listed for the huntPilotDN, if any.  This field is one of those created by the groups lookup, which runs automatically once created.  See 'App Settings > Groups and extensions' to learn more.
Specifies the partition for the hunt pilot DN.
This field is populated only when the HuntPilot member answers the call which is placed either directly or as a result of redirection to the huntPilot.

(added by the app) This will contain the subgroup that is listed for the huntPilotDN, if any.  This field is one of those created by the groups lookup, which runs automatically once created. See 'App Settings > Groups and extensions' to learn more.
Interval Conceal Ratio - an interval-based average concealment rate that is the ratio of concealment time over speech time for the last 3 seconds of active speech.
Interval Conceal Ratio Max represents the maximum concealment ratio that is observed during the call.
Carrier ID of the trunk group through which the call arrived or the partnering voice service provider identifier of the incoming VoIP call.
Description assigned to the voice port of the incoming call.
Trunk group label associated with the group of voice ports from which the incoming TDM call arrived on the gateway.
Gatekeeper identifier, or the source zone or area, of the incoming VoIP call.
(added by the app)  Used by the app internally to correlate CallManager CDR records with CUBE CDR records.  This is extracted from the ProtocolCallRefId fields
Identifies the globally unique call reference identification for the protocol. The value is received from the upstream voice product. The value is alphanumeric and truncated to 32 characters.

This field identifies the protocol (SIP, H.323, CTI/JTAPI, or Q.931) used between Cisco Unified CM and the upstream voice product in the call path. (0 = Unknown, 1 = SIP, 2 = H323, 3 = CTI/JTAPI, 4 = Q931)
(added by the app) English Language version of the protocol (SIP, H.323, CTI/JTAPI, or Q.931) used between Cisco Unified CM and the upstream voice product in the call path.

Type of information carried by media. 1=Other 9 not described, 2=Speech, 3=UnrestrictedDigital, 4=UnrestrictedDigital56 (Unrestricted Digital with 56k rate), 5=RestrictedDigital, 6=Audio31 (3.1 kHz audio), 7=Audio, 8=Video, 9=PacketSwitched, 10=FAX.

(added by the app) This represents whether the very first call leg was internal, incoming, outgoing or tandem.  With very complex call flow, an incoming call might then have a number of internal legs later on.   This field can therefore be more useful as a split-by or group-by field, since it will always be single-valued

(added by the app) This represents the group of the user associated with the finalCalledPartyNumber on the initial call leg in a multi leg call. For single-leg calls it will simply be the group of the user associated with the finalCalledPartyNumber.

(added by the app) This represents the name of the user associated with the finalCalledPartyNumber on the initial call leg in a multi leg call. For single-leg calls it will simply be the name of the user associated with the finalCalledPartyNumber.
(added by the app) This represents the finalCalledPartyNumber of the first call leg in a multi leg call. For single-leg calls it will simply be the finalCalledPartyNumber itself

(added by the app) This represents the subgroup of the user associated with the finalCalledPartyNumber on the initial call leg in a multi leg call. For single-leg calls it will simply be the subgroup of the user associated with the finalCalledPartyNumber.

(added by the app) This represents the group of the user associated with the callingPartyNumber on the initial call leg in a multi leg call. For single-leg calls it will simply be the group of the user associated with the callingPartyNumber.

(added by the app) This represents the name of the user associated with the callingPartyNumber on the initial call leg in a multi leg call. For single-leg calls it will simply be the name of the user associated with the callingPartyNumber.
(added by the app) This represents the callingPartyNumber of the first call leg in a multi leg call. For single-leg calls it will simply be the callingPartyNumber itself

(added by the app) This represents the subgroup of the user associated with the callingPartyNumber on the initial call leg in a multi leg call. For single-leg calls it will simply be the subgroup of the user associated with the callingPartyNumber.
(added by the app) This field will combine the values of origDeviceName and destDeviceName but only for non-gateway devices. Useful for doing device utilization reports.
(added by the app) This field will combine the values of orig_device_type and dest_device_type but only for non-gateway devices. Useful for doing device utilization reports.
Cause of failed calls.

(added by the app) This field will hold the party number(s) on the internal side of each call leg.  In other words for internal call legs it will have callingPartyNumber, originalCalledPartyNumber AND finalCalledPartyNumber.
(added by the app) This field represents the union of all the ip address fields in the given calls and call legs.
Maximum number of hops in the SIP invite message.

An estimate of the statistical variance of the RTP data packet interarrival time, measured in milliseconds and expressed as an unsigned integer. The interarrival jitter J specifies the mean deviation (smoothed absolute value) of the difference D in packet spacing at the receiver, compared to the sender for a pair of packets. RFC 1889 contains detailed computation algorithms. The value remains zero if the connection was set in 'send only' mode.
The reason for a join. For example, if the join takes place on behalf of a transfer, the OnBehalfOf code specifies '10'.
(added by the app) The english names of the OnBehalfOf code specified in the call leg's joinOnBehalfOf value.

For forwarded calls, This field specifies the phone number of the next to last hop before the call reaches its final destination. If only one hop occurs, this number matches the OriginalCalledPartyNumber. For calls that are not forwarded, this field matches the OriginalCalledPartyNumber and the FinalCalledPartyNumber. For calls to a conference bridge, this field contains the actual identifier of the conference bridge, which is an alphanumeric string (for example, b0019901001). If the call is never redirected, this field remains empty.

Identifies the partition name that is associated with the LastRedirectDn field because Cisco Unified Communications Manager supports multiple Cisco Unified IP Phones with the same extension number in different partitions. For calls that egress through an H.323 gateway, this field specifies the partition name that is associated with the route pattern that points to the gateway.
This is the pattern matched by the lastRedirectDn field.
This field is used with CUCM's external call control feature and represents the reason why the call was intercepted for the last time.
(added by the app) This is the name corresponding to the lastRedirectingRoutingReason code.  ie lastRedirectingRoutingReason 5 corresponds to PDPDecision_Forward_Fulfilled.
(added by the app) This is the readable description of the code specified in the lastRedirectRedirectReason field.
The reason for redirection of the last redirected party. For example, if the last redirected party was redirected on behalf of a conference, the OnBehalfOf code specifies '4'.
(added by the app) The readable name of the reason why the last redirected party was redirected. See the field 'lastRedirectRedirectOnBehalfOf'.
The last redirect reason for redirection.
Number of received voice packets that arrived too late to play out with the codec during the call.

This field designates value that is an estimate of the network latency, expressed in milliseconds. This value represents the average value of the difference between the NTP timestamp that the RTP Control Protocol (RTCP) messages indicates and the NTP timestamp of the receivers, measured when these messages are received. Cisco Unified Communications Manager obtains the average by summing all estimates then dividing by the number of RTCP messages that have been received. For detailed information, see RFC 1889.

Experimental field that is generated on call legs that have an eventtype whose name begins with 'leg_type_'. The value of this field is taken from the rest of the eventtype name after the 'leg_type_' prefix.  Note that we only ship a few of these definitions - currently 2 for voicemail and one for transfers to Contact Center.  You may create these yourself simply by creating an eventtype whose name brings with 'leg_type_', and using the Health Checks to make sure that no call legs ever end up with more than one leg_type matching.
(added by the app) This field will count the number of call legs, including but not limited to any transfers.
Local hostname that would be accessed or used by the SNMP MIBs.
ifIndex value of the logical interface through which this call was made. For ISDN media, this is the ifIndex of the B channel that was used for this call.
Number of lost voice packets during the call.
Low-water mark voice playout FIFO delay during the voice call.
Maximum bandwidth used by the video call.
(added by the app) this is the minute of the hour at which the call leg occurred, as expressed in the user's current timezone

MOS Listening Quality K-factor -- an estimate of the MOS score of the last 8 seconds of speech on the reception signal path.  Note Cisco has switched to recommending CS/SCS/CSR and SCSR as more reliable and consistent quality metrics.  See the SCSR_overall and CSR_overall fields.
The running average of MLQK scores that are observed since the beginning of a call.
The minimum MLQK score that is observed since the beginning of a call and represents the worst sounding 8-second interval.
The maximum MLQK score that is observed since the beginning of a call and represents the best sounding 8-second interval.
Another MOS score statistic introduced in more recent CUCM versions, it's unclear what this actually is, as Cisco describes it nowhere in the callmanager docs for any version.
For mobility calls, specifies the callingPartyNumber.
An integer valued code specifying the mobility feature that is invoked for this mobile call.
(added by the app) Specifies the name of the mobility feature that was invoked for this mobile call.
(added by the app) This field represents the current month of the year as calculated in the timezone of the Splunk server.

(added by the groups lookup) This field represents the union of the callingPartyName, originalCalledPartyName and finalCalledPartyName, as looked up in the groups lookup, for the given call leg or for all call legs in the given call.
This field specifies the server, or node within the Cisco Unified Communications Manager cluster, where this record gets generated.
Average noise level for the call, in dBm.

(added by the app UI) This field represents the union of the values found in callingPartyNumber, originalCalledPartyNumber and finalCalledPartyNumber. This is useful for reporting when you want to include statistics from the number regardless of whether it was present as the calling party or one of the called parties.

The total number of payload octets (that is, not including header or padding) that the device has received in RTP data packets since starting reception on this connection. The count includes packets that are received from different sources if this is a multicast call. The value remains zero if the connection is set in 'send only' mode.

The total number of payload octets (that is, not including header or padding) that the device transmits in RTP data packets since starting transmission on this connection. The value remains zero if the connection is set to 'receive nly' mode.

The total number of RTP data packets that have been lost since the beginning of reception. This number designates the number of packets that were expected, less the number of packets that were actually received, where the number of packets that were received includes any that are late or duplicates. Thus, packets that arrive late do not get counted as lost, and the loss may be negative if duplicate packets exist. The number of packets that are expected designates the extended last sequence number that was received, as defined next, less the initial sequence number that was received. The value remains zero if the connection was set in 'send only' mode. For detailed information, see RFC 1889.

The total number of RTP data packets that the device has received since starting reception on this connection. The count includes packets that are received from different sources if this is a multicast call. The value remains zero if the connection is set in 'send only' mode.

The total number of Routing Table Protocol (RTP) data packets that the device transmits since starting transmission on this connection. The value remains zero if the connection is set to 'receive only' mode.
The total number of payload octets (that is, not including header or padding) received in RTP data packets by the device since starting reception on this connection.
The total number of payload octets (that is, not including header or padding) transmitted in RTP data packets by the device since starting transmission on this connection.
The total number of RTP data packets that have been lost since the beginning of reception on this connection.
The total number of RTP data packets received by the device since starting reception on this connection.
The total number of RTP data packets transmitted by the device since starting transmission on this connection.
(added by the app) For call legs that terminate when one party goes on-hook,  this field will have the value 'caller' or 'recipient' depending on which party went on-hook.
Duration, in ms, of voice playout from data received on time for this call.
(added by the app) A flag that may or may not be present on an MGCP gateway value, if present indicating that the type of endpoint is analog
(added by the app) When origination devices involve UCCX this will contain the origination route point.

(added by the app) This gives you the type of device originating the call. Possible values are hardphone, jabberphone, softphone, uccx. If the device does not match one of the extractions in transforms.conf then the field will be undefined.
(added by the app) This is the name of the gateway used in the inbound call
(added by the app) One of the components of how MGCP gateways are listed - taken together port slot and sub_unit define an individual interface on the given MGCP gateway.
(added by the app) One of the components of how MGCP gateways are listed - taken together port slot and sub_unit define an individual interface on the given MGCP gateway.
(added by the app) One of the components of how MGCP gateways are listed - taken together port slot and sub_unit define an individual interface on the given MGCP gateway.
(added by the app) The MGCP gateway name itself, stripped of the AALN, port slot and sub_unit components.
(added by the app) this is the mac address extracted from originating hardphone devices.
Specifies the bandwidth in kbps of the originating device's codec, if known. (to check manually, look at the calls origAudioCodec and then look that row up in codec_types.csv within the app)
(added by the app) Represents the audio codec used by the originating device.
(added by the app) This field is extracted by default from devices whose names begin with CCG.
(added by the app) The description as per Cisco's docs, of the corresponding origCalledPartyRedirectReason code.

Call release cause code specifying the reason for redirection of the original called party. For example, if the original called party was redirected because of a conference, the OnBehalfOf code specifies '4'.
(added by the app) The description as per Cisco's docs, of the corresponding origCalledPartyRedirectOnBehalfOf code.
The reason for a redirect of the original called party.
(added by the app) If the origDeviceName value is present in the devices lookup, this field will be the callingSearchSpace listed for that device.

Call release cause code specifying why the originator was terminated. For example, if the originator of the call hangs up the phone, the OnBehalfOf code shows '12' for Device. If the call terminates because of a transfer, the OnBehalfOf code shows '10' for Transfer.
(added by the app) The readable name of the reason why the originator was terminated. See the field 'origCallTerminationOnBehalfOf'.
This field is added by the app and is the english description of the appropriate call release cause code for calls cleared by the originating party

For clearing causes that are received over ISDN signaling links, this field specifies the Location field that is indicated in the ISDN release message. For clearing causes that are created internally by the Cisco Unified Communications Manager, this value specifies zero.
For calls that are cleared by the originating party, this integer reflects the reason for clearance. For calls that are cleared by the terminating party, this value will be zero.

(added by the app) For calls whose origDeviceName matches one of the subnets in the Sites lookup,   this field holds the value of the matching subnet.  This can be useful when you have multiple subnets mapped to a single sites,   but where the individual subnets themselves remain occasionally interesting. You can add and edit subnets in the Sites lookup under 'App Settings > Sites'.

The conference ID that is associated with the originating leg of the call. In most cases, this field equals 0. For conference chaining scenarios, the origConversationId and destConversationId fields identify which conferences are chained together.
(added by the app) If the origDeviceName value is present in the devices lookup, this field will be the description listed for that device.
(added by the app) If the origDeviceName value is present in the devices lookup, this field will be the primary (or failing that secondary) DN or DiD number associated with the oiriginating device.
The name of the originating device, whether it's a soft phone, physical phone or gateway device.
(added by the app) If the origDeviceName value is present in the devices lookup, this field will be the devicePool listed for that device.
(added by the app) If the origDeviceName value is present in the devices lookup, this field will be the protocol listed for that device.

The DTMF method that the originator uses. (0 - No DTMF - Use ANY matched DTMF. 1 - OOB - Use OOB if endpoints behind SIPTrunk support it. 2 - 2833 - Use RFC2833 if endpoints behind SIPTrunk support it. 3 - OOB and 2833 - Use both KPML and RFC2833 if endpoints behind SIPTrunk can support both. 4 - Unknown).  See field origDTMFMethod_description also.
(added by the app) The human readable version of the field OrigDTMFMethod.
(added by the app) Extracted from the originating device names that match the 'cisco-cdr-orighardphone' regex in transforms.conf, this represents the username of the originating hardphone.

(added by the app) This will contain the department or group in which the originalCalledPartyNumber is listed.  This field is one of those created by the groups lookup, which runs automatically once created.  See 'Define Groups' under Settings to learn more.

(added by the app) This will contain the name of the person with the given originalCalledPartyNumber.  This field is one of those created by the groups lookup, which runs automatically once created.  See 'App Settings > Groups and extensions' to learn more.

Number or extension to which the original call was presented, prior to any forwarding. If translation rules are configured, this number reflects the called number after the translations have been applied.

Identifies the partition name that is associated with the OriginalCalledPartyNumber field because Cisco Unified Communications Manager supports multiple Cisco Unified IP Phones with the same extension number in different partitions. For calls that egress through an H.323 gateway, this field uniquely specifies the partition name that is associated with the route pattern that points to the gateway.
This is the pattern matched by the originalCalledPartyNumber.

(created by the app, if the groups lookup has been configured to). See the documentation under Setup>Groups. If you set up the groups lookup to have this field in addition to originalCalledPartyGroup, then this field will exist
Sent in start and stop accounting messages for call legs 1 and 4.

(modified by the app) The IP address of the device that originated the call signaling. For Cisco Unified IP Phones, this field specifies the address of the phone. For PSTN calls, this field specifies the address of the H.323 gateway. For intercluster calls, this field specifies the address of the remote Cisco Unified Communications Manager.  Although present in the CDR as an integer in most callmanager versions, the Sideview app automatically converts these values to real IP addresses.
(added by the app) Extracted from the originating device names that match the 'cisco-cdr-origipcom' regex in transforms.conf, this represents the username of the originating IP Communicator device.

This field identifies the IP address of the device that originates the call signalling. The field can be either IPv4 or IPv6 format depending on the type of IP address that gets used for the call.  For Cisco Unified IP Phones, this field is the address of the Cisco Unified IP Phone. For PSTN calls, this field is the address of the gateway. For intercluster calls, this field is the address of the remote Cisco Unified Communications Manager.
(added by the app) Extracted from the originating device names that match the 'cisco-cdr-origjabberphone' regex in transforms.conf, this represents the username of the originating Jabber device.

This field identifies the originating leg of a call. Be aware that this value is unique within a cluster. If the leg of a call persists across several sub-calls, and consequently several CDRs (as during a call transfer), this value remains constant.
The media bandwidth that is used at the origination of the call.
This field is not reported by CUCM

This field identifies the number of milliseconds of data per packet that the originating party sends. This field normally gets set to 10, 20, or 30 for G.729 or G.711 codecs, but the field can store any nonzero value.
The codec type that the originator uses to transmit media. CUCM currently uses the following payload capability values: 0, 1-16, 18-20, 25, 32, 33, 81-86. See 'Codec Types' in CUCM docs.

IP address of the device that originates the media for the call. For Cisco Unified IP Phones, this field specifies the address of the phone.For PSTN calls, this field specifies the address of the H.323 gateway. For intercluster calls, this field specifies the address of the remote phone.
This field identifies the IP port number that is associated with the OrigMediaTransportAddress_IP field.
For mobility calls, specifies the originating mobile device's call duration.
For mobility calls, specifies the originating mobile device name.
This field identifies the server, or node within a cluster, to which the originator of the call is registered at the time that the call is made.
For MLPP, the precedence level of the original call leg.  (0=FLASH OVERRIDE/ EXECUTIVE OVERRIDE, 1=FLASH,2=IMMEDIATE,3=PRIORITY,4=ROUTINE ).  See field origPrecendenceLevel_description also.
(added by the app) The human readable version of the origPrecendenceLevel.  FLASH OVERRIDE, EXECUTIVE OVERRIDE, FLASH, IMMEDIATE, PRIORITY, ROUTINE
(added by the app) If the origDeviceName value is present in the devices lookup, this field will be the product name listed for that device.
This field is used with CUCM's external call control feature and represents the reason why the call was intercepted for the first time.
(added by the app) This is the name corresponding to the origRoutingReason code.  ie origRoutingReason 5 corresponds to PDPDecision_Forward_Fulfilled.
Status of the RSVP audio reservation from originator to terminator.  See field origRSVPAudioStat_description for more information and a human readable version of these values.

(added by the app) The human readable version of the Status of the RSVP audio reservation from originator to terminator.  0 - No reservation,1 - RSVP Reservation Failure condition at call setup or feature invocation, 2  - RSVP Reservation Success condition at call setup or feature invocation,3 - RSVP Reservation No Response (RSVP Agent) condition at call setup or feature invocation,4 - RSVP Mid Call Failure Preempted condition (preempted after call setup),5 - RSVP Mid Call Failure Lost Bandwidth condition (includes all mid-call failures except MLPP preemption).
Status of the RSVP video reservation from originator to terminator.  See field origRSVPVideoStat_description for more information and a human readable version of these values.

(added by the app) The human readable version of the Status of the RSVP video reservation from originator to terminator.  0 - No reservation,1 - RSVP Reservation Failure condition at call setup or feature invocation, 2  - RSVP Reservation Success condition at call setup or feature invocation,3 - RSVP Reservation No Response (RSVP Agent) condition at call setup or feature invocation,4 - RSVP Mid Call Failure Preempted condition (preempted after call setup),5 - RSVP Mid Call Failure Lost Bandwidth condition (includes all mid-call failures except MLPP preemption).
(added by the app) If the origDeviceName value is present in the devices lookup, this field will be the security profile listed for that device.

(added by the app) This field is derived from matching the origIpAddr against the subnets specified in the app's Sites lookup.  You can add and edit subnets in the Sites lookup under 'App Settings > Sites'.

For calls that originate at a gateway, this field indicates the B-channel number of the T1, PRI, or BRI trunk where the call originates, or a zero value for FXS or FXO trunks.  For H.323 gateways, the span number remains unknown, and this field contains the call leg ID of the originator.For calls that did not originate at a gateway, the value specifies zero.
(added by the app) Extracted from the originating device names that match the 'cisco-cdr-origuccx' regex in transforms.conf, this represents the username of the originating UCCX device.
(added by the app) Extracted from the originating device names that match the 'cisco-cdr-origunityvm' regex in transforms.conf, this represents the username of the originating Unity Voicemail device.
(added by the app) If the devices lookup has been set up (Generally with Sideview's Supporting Add On for Cisco AXL), this will populate with the department of the given party
(added by the app) If the devices lookup has been set up (Generally with Sideview's Supporting Add On for Cisco AXL), this will populate with the email address of the given party
(added by the app) If the devices lookup has been set up (Generally with Sideview's Supporting Add On for Cisco AXL), this will populate with the userid of the given party
(added by the app) If the devices lookup has been set up (Generally with Sideview's Supporting Add On for Cisco AXL), this will populate with the username of the given party
Specifies the bandwidth in kbps of the originating device's codec, if known. (to check manually, look at the call's origVideoCodec and then look that row up in codec_types.csv within the app)
The bandwidth that is measured in units of kbps.

Bandwidth measured in kbps for the second video channel.  Default - 0. If media does not get established, this field displays 0. Also, if H.239 and BFCP are not supported for this call, this field displays 0.
The codec type that the originator uses to transmit video. (100 = H.261,101 = H.263,102 = Vieo)

The codec type that the originator uses to transmit video (H.261, H.263, or H.264) for the second video channel.  Default - 0. If media does not get established, this field displays 0. Also, if H.239 and BFCP are not supported for this call, this field displays 0.

Indicates the transmitting resolution. In the case of H.264 codec or SIP device, this field refers to the max transmitting resolution the device can transmit for this call. See field origVideoCap_Resolution_description for more information and human-readable values.

Indicates the transmitting resolution for the second video channel. In the case of H.264 codec or SIP device, this field refers to the maximum transmitting resolution the device can transmit for this call.  \Default - 0. If media is not established, this field stays 0. Also, if H.239 and BFCP are not supported for this call, this field displays 0.   For more information and human-readable values, see field origVideoCap_Resolution_Channel2_description

(added by the app) This field identifies the video resolution for the second video channel. (0 = Nothing/default, 1 = SQCIF, 2 = QCIF, 3 = CIF, 4 = CIF4, 5 = CIF16, 6 = H263 custom resolution, 7 = W360P, 8 = VGA, 9 = W448P, 10 = HD720P, 11 = HD1080P, 12 = CIF2 )

(added by the app) The human-readable version of the video resolution. (0 = Nothing/default, 1 = SQCIF, 2 = QCIF, 3 = CIF, 4 = CIF4, 5 = CIF16, 6 = H263 custom resolution, 7 = W360P, 8 = VGA, 9 = W448P, 10 = HD720P, 11 = HD1080P, 12 = CIF2 )

Identifies the H.239 video channel role of the device that receives the call.  Default - 0. If media does not get established, this field displays 0. Also, if H.239 is not supported, this field displays 0.
For video calls, represents the codec used by the originating device. For audio calls this field will be empty.
The IP address of the device that originates the call.

Identifies the v4 IP address of the device that originates the call for the second video channel.  Default - 0. If media does not get established, this field displays 0. Also, if H.239 and BFCP are not supported for this call, this field displays 0.
The video RTP port that is associated with the origVideoTransportAddress_IP field.

Identifies the video RTP port associated with the origH239VideoTransportAddress_IP field for the second video channel.  Default - 0. If media does not get established, this field displays 0. Also, if H.239 and BFCP are not supported for this call, this field displays 0.
Carrier ID of the trunk group through which the call leaves the gateway or the partnering voice services provider identifier of the outgoing VoIP call.
Description assigned to the voice port of the outgoing call.
Trunk-group label associated with the group of voice ports from which the outgoing TDM call leaves the gateway.
Gatekeeper identifier, or the destination zone or area, of the outgoing VoIP call.
(added by the app) this is a guid that can be used in some advanced cases to follow links to downstream voice systems.
Identifies the globally unique call reference identification for the protocol. The value is passed to the next downstream voiced product. The value is alphanumeric and truncated to 32 characters.

This field identifies the protocol (SIP, H.323, CTI/JTAPI, or Q.931) used between Cisco Unified CM and the downstream voice product in the call path. (0 = Unknown, 1 = SIP, 2 = H323, 3 = CTI/JTAPI, 4 = Q931)
(added by the app) English language version of the protocol (SIP, H.323, CTI/JTAPI, or Q.931) used between Cisco Unified CM and the downstream voice product in the call path.

The called party number gets outpulsed from the device. This field gets populated only when normalization or localization takes place at the device. Values are alphanumeric strings of up to 50 characters.

The calling party number gets outpulsed from the device. This field gets populated only when normalization or localization takes place at the device.
