Values are alphanumeric strings of up to 50 characters. NOTE: YOUR DATA MAY NOT HAVE ANY VALUES FOR THIS FIELD. IF THAT IS THE CASE THESE EXAMPLE REPORTS WILL NOT WORK.
(added by the app) The total kb used for this call leg.  Equals audio_leg_kb plus video_leg_kb.
Override session time.
Total number of packets received.
Total number of transmitted packets.
Number that this call was connected to in E.164 format.
ID value of the peer table entry to which this call was made. If a peer table entry for this call does not exist, the value of this object is zero.
ifIndex value of the peer table entry to which this call was made. If a peer table entry for this call does not exist, the value of this object is zero.
Subaddress configured under a dial peer.
(added by the app) this is a guid that can be used in some advanced cases to follow links to/from ICM.
(added by the app) this is a guid that can be used in some advanced cases to follow links to/from ICM.
A text string that the database uses internally to uniquely identify each row. Cisco claims that this text string provides no meaning to the call itself.
(added by the app) This represents the union of both the origProductName and destProductName fields.
(added by the app) this is the union of the IncomingProtocolCallRef and OutgoingProtocolCallRef fields, with some cleanup occurring to remove emptystring values.

(added by the app) This field is calculated for calls that have quality statistics and its values can be good, acceptable, fair or poor. Thresholds can be defined based on the most common UCM quality fields. For more details navigate to 'App Settings > Quality thresholds'.
Average playout FIFO delay plus the decoder delay during the voice call.

Redirecting number extracted from the redirect number parameter. Sent in start accounting messages for all call legs. noa=Nature of address, npi=Numbering plan indicato, rpi=Presentation indicator, #=Address of the redirecting number
Remote-media gateway IP address.
Remote-media gateway DNS name.
Remote-media gateway UDP port.
Remote system UDP listener port to which voice packets are transmitted.

(added by the app) This field infers the number of seconds of ring time on the call leg, regardless of whether it connected or not.  For calls that connect, it will be the number of seconds between the origination and connect timestamps.  For calls that never connect (where connect time is 0 in the CDR),  it is the number of seconds between the origination and disconnect timestamps
Voice-packet round-trip delay, in ms, between local and remote devices on the IP backbone during a call.
Identifies the actual bandwidth used for the received video stream.
Identifies the maximum negotiated bandwidth for the received video stream.
Identifies the type of video codec used for received video stream.
Identifies the average frame rate measured in frames per second for the received video stream.
Identifies the resolution of the received video stream (for example, 640x480).

Severely Concealed Secs represents the time during which a significant amount of concealment is observed. If the concealment that is observed is usually greater than 50 milliseconds or approximately 5 percent, the speech probably does not seem very audible.
(added by the app) This is calculated from the SCS values and is just the total SCS across all of the given call's CMR data. When the call has only one CMR then SCS_total and SCS are the same.

(added by the app) this is the CS Ratio, in other words the seconds of severely concealed speech divided by the total number of seconds. Note that this is calculated by the app and not on the individual CMR records but across all CMR's for the given call, as a single statistic on the call. It is calculated by taking SCS_total and dividing by duration_total.
(added by the app) Only defined for calls that are answered, this is the number of seconds between origination time and connect time

(added by the app) An advanced field that can be useful in some unusual cases, this is the number of seconds between origination time and the disconnect time. Note that the duration fields are much more useful, and they are the number of seconds between the *connect* times and the disconnect times.
(added by the app) This represents the union of both the origSecurityProfile and destSecurityProfile fields.

(added by the app) for CUCM cdr this is extracted from the ingested filename, and it is the first segment seen after cdr|cmr.  This is also notably extracted from CUBE CDR and is the first segment in the filename after 'cube'.
Gatekeeper-related.
Session protocol used for calls between the local and remote router through the IP backbone. Always equal to 'sip' for SIP or 'Cisco' for H.323.
(added by the app) This field combines the values of origSite and destSite into one multivalued field.
A Splunk field indicating the filename of the source data.
A Splunk field indicating the sourcetype.
(added by the app) this is the CIM-compliant version of the origIPAddr field.

(added by the groups lookup) This field represents the union of the callingPartySubgroup, originalCalledPartySubgroup and finalCalledPartySubgroup, as looked up in the groups lookup, for the given call leg or for all call legs in the given call.
T1/channel associated signaling (CAS) or E1/R2 signal information about a subscriber.
Transferrer information in the REFER/BYE/ALSO of SIP call. Used only in SIP call transfer.

(added by the app) This represents the group of the called party on the final (aka 'terminating') call leg in a multi leg call. For single-leg calls it will simply be the group of the user associated with the finalCalledPartyNumber.

(added by the app) This represents the name of the called party on the final (aka 'terminating') call leg in a multi leg call. For single-leg calls it will simply be the name of the user associated with the finalCalledPartyNumber.
(added by the app) This represents the finalCalledPartyNumber of the terminating call leg in a multi leg call. For single-leg calls it will simply be the finalCalledPartyNumber itself

(added by the app) This represents the subgroup of the called party on the final (aka 'terminating') call leg in a multi leg call. For single-leg calls it will simply be the subgroup of the user associated with the finalCalledPartyNumber.

(added by the app) This represents the group of the calling party on the final (aka 'terminating') call leg in a multi leg call. For single-leg calls it will simply be the group of the user associated with the callingPartyNumber.

(added by the app) This represents the name of the calling party on the final (aka 'terminating') call leg in a multi leg call. For single-leg calls it will simply be the name of the user associated with the callingPartyNumber.
(added by the app) This represents the callingPartyNumber of the terminating call leg in a multi leg call. For single-leg calls it will simply be the callingPartyNumber itself.

(added by the app) This represents the subgroup of the calling party on the final (aka 'terminating') call leg in a multi leg call. For single-leg calls it will simply be the subgroup of the user associated with the callingPartyNumber.

(added by the app) The start time of the call.  Note that how this is calculated varies across the different sourcetypes.  For instance in cucm_cdr this is the dateTimeOrigination of the call's first leg. For cube_cdr it is the unix-time field, an epochtime-valued field that is always the first field in the row.

(added by the app) only present in particular views). This field is created by the app in certain views and represents a fairly intelligent merging of location information deduced from the country code,  area code and exchange, with other information deduced from the IP addresses and relevant subnet from the sites lookup. Users wishing to use this field in their own searches and reports can use this combination of macros to construct the field manually in their data - `get_sites` | `get_locations` | `get_to_and_from`
Specifies how long in seconds a caller spent in a queue.

(added by the app) This field will count the number of transfers. Specifically a single leg call will have transfers=0,  a call with two or more call legs with one call leg showing 'Call Split' will have transfers=1,  a call with three or more call legs with two legs showing 'Call Split' will have transfers=2 etc.
Sent in start and stop accounting records for call legs 1 and 4.
Duration, in ms, of transmit path open from this peer to the voice gateway for the call.
Identifies the actual bandwidth that is used for the transmitted video stream.
Identifies the maximum negotiated bandwidth for the transmitted video stream.
Identifies the type of video codec used for the transmitted video stream.
Identifies the average frame rate that is measured in frames per second for the transmitted video stream.
Identifies the resolution of the transmitted video stream (for example, 640x480).
(added by the app) This field tells you whether the call is an incoming, outgoing, internal or tandem call. It is calculated from the presence of the orig_gateway and/or dest_gateway fields.
System time stamp when CDR is captured.
(added by the app) This represents the union of both the origUserDepartment and destUserDepartment fields.
(added by the app) This represents the union of both the origUserEmail and destUserEmail fields.
(added by the app) This represents the union of both the origUserId and destUserId fields.
Username for authentication. Usually this is the same as the calling number.
(added by the app) This represents the union of both the origUserName and destUserName fields.
Whether or not voice-activity detection (VAD) is enabled for the voice call.
(added by the app) The total kb used for the video portion of this call leg.
a CMR field that we believe is only present in very old CUCM versions. Contact us if you see this field in your data.
a CMR field that we believe is only present in very old CUCM versions. Contact us if you see this field in your data.
a CMR field that we believe is only present in very old CUCM versions. Contact us if you see this field in your data.
a CMR field that we believe is only present in very old CUCM versions. Contact us if you see this field in your data.
a CMR field that we believe is only present in very old CUCM versions. Contact us if you see this field in your data.
a CMR field that we believe is only present in very old CUCM versions. Contact us if you see this field in your data.
Type of feature: BXFER = Blind transfer, CFA = Call forward all, CFBY = Call forward busy, CFNA = Call forward no answer, CXFER = Consultative transfer, TWC = Two-way call.
Duration, in ms, for this call.
Specifies whether the call was put into a queue or not.  1 indicates yes,  0 indicates no.
